display.c(3) Library Functions Manual display.c(3)

NAME

display.c - Display width and centering utilities implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int display_width (const char *text)
int display_center_horizontal (const char *text, int terminal_width)
int display_center_vertical (int content_height, int terminal_height)
int display_height (const char *text, int terminal_width)

Detailed Description

Display width and centering utilities implementation.

Definition in file lib/util/display.c.

Function Documentation

int display_center_horizontal (const char * text, int terminal_width)

Definition at line 32 of file lib/util/display.c.

32                                                                     {
33   if (!text || terminal_width <= 0) {
34     return 0;
35   }
36 
37   int text_width = display_width(text);
38 
39   // If text is empty or wider than terminal, don't add padding
40   if (text_width == 0 || text_width >= terminal_width) {
41     return 0;
42   }
43 
44   // Calculate left padding to center
45   return (terminal_width - text_width) / 2;
46 }




References display_width().






int
  display_center_vertical (int content_height, int terminal_height)


Definition at line 48 of file
  lib/util/display.c.


48                                                                      {
49   if (content_height <= 0 || terminal_height <= 0) {
50     return 0;
51   }
52 
53   if (content_height >= terminal_height) {
54     return 0;
55   }
56 
57   return (terminal_height - content_height) / 2;
58 }










int
  display_height (const char * text, int terminal_width)


Definition at line 60 of file
  lib/util/display.c.


60                                                          {
61   if (!text || terminal_width <= 0) {
62     return 0;
63   }
64 
65   int total_lines = 0;
66   const char *p = text;
67 
68   // Count lines by processing the text and counting newlines + final segment
69   while (*p) {
70     // Find the end of this line (next newline or end of string)
71     const char *line_end = p;
72     while (*line_end && *line_end != '\n') {
73       line_end++;
74     }
75 
76     // Calculate display width of this line
77     size_t line_len = line_end - p;
78     if (line_len > 0) {
79       // Strip ANSI codes and calculate display width
80       char *stripped = ansi_strip_escapes(p, line_len);
81       if (stripped) {
82         int line_width = utf8_display_width(stripped);
83         SAFE_FREE(stripped);
84 
85         if (line_width > 0) {
86           // Calculate how many display lines this wraps to
87           int display_lines = (line_width + terminal_width - 1) / terminal_width; // Ceiling division
88           total_lines += display_lines;
89         } else {
90           total_lines += 1;
91         }
92       } else {
93         // If stripping fails, count as 1 line
94         total_lines += 1;
95       }
96     } else {
97       // Empty line still counts as 1 line
98       total_lines += 1;
99     }
100 
101     // Move to next line
102     if (*line_end == '\n') {
103       p = line_end + 1;
104     } else {
105       break;
106     }
107   }
108 
109   return total_lines;
110 }






References ansi_strip_escapes(), and
    utf8_display_width().


Referenced by terminal_screen_render().






int
  display_width (const char * text)


Definition at line 13 of file
  lib/util/display.c.


13                                     {
14   if (!text) {
15     return 0;
16   }
17 
18   // Strip ANSI escape codes
19   char *stripped = ansi_strip_escapes(text, strlen(text));
20 
21   // Use stripped version if available, otherwise use original text
22   const char *to_measure = stripped ? stripped : text;
23 
24   // Calculate display width using UTF-8 aware calculation
25   int width = utf8_display_width(to_measure);
26   SAFE_FREE(stripped);
27 
28   // Return 0 for empty or invalid input, otherwise return calculated width
29   return width < 0 ? 0 : width;
30 }






References ansi_strip_escapes(), and
    utf8_display_width().


Referenced by display_center_horizontal(),
    frame_buffer_render_centered(), layout_print_two_column_row(),
    terminal_fd_reader_next(), and terminal_screen_render().
